name: Lefkovitz & Lefkovitz
description_short: Nashville and Cookeville bankruptcy law firm serving Middle Tennessee with Chapter 7, Chapter 11, and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings plus debt relief services.
description_long:
Lefkovitz & Lefkovitz is a bankruptcy and debt relief law firm based in Nashville with an additional office in Cookeville, Tennessee, serving clients throughout Middle Tennessee and the Upper Cumberland region. The firm has established itself by handling both straightforward and complex bankruptcy cases for individuals and businesses over many years of practice.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 bankruptcy (liquidation), Chapter 13 bankruptcy (reorganization), and Chapter 11 bankruptcy (business reorganization). They also provide debt relief and debt management consulting. According to their website, they guide clients through the entire bankruptcy process step-by-step and claim to have helped thousands of people resolve their financial problems. The firm emphasizes willingness to handle cases that other attorneys decline, particularly complex matters involving multiple businesses, million-dollar debts, and litigation.

What distinguishes Lefkovitz & Lefkovitz is their stated philosophy of creative problem-solving and their track record with difficult cases. Their website notes that hundreds of clients have come to them saying "I've been told this can't be done," and the firm claims to have found solutions in those instances. Client testimonials emphasize their competence, honesty about case outcomes, professionalism, and personalized attention—including willingness to go "the extra mile" on non-straightforward cases. Multiple reviews note the firm's experience and dedication.

As a bankruptcy law firm, the primary caveat is that bankruptcy should only be pursued after genuine exploration of alternatives, as it carries significant long-term credit consequences. While the firm emphasizes honest counsel about what clients need, potential clients should understand that bankruptcy is a legal process requiring court involvement, and outcomes depend heavily on individual circumstances and whether cases qualify under Chapter 7, 13, or 11 provisions.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Handles both simple and complex bankruptcy cases, including Chapter 7, 13, and 11 filings
Explicitly accepts difficult cases that other attorneys decline, particularly multi-business and high-debt situations
Two office locations (Nashville and Cookeville) serving broader Middle Tennessee region
Client testimonials consistently praise honesty about case outcomes and process expectations
Firm claims to have helped thousands of people achieve debt discharge and financial fresh starts
Personalized attention with attorneys willing to spend time on non-straightforward cases
Multiple attorney reviews from other legal professionals attesting to competence and professionalism
cons: Bankruptcy carries severe long-term credit consequences (typically 7-10 years on credit reports) not fully detailed on their site
No pricing information or fee structures disclosed on website—clients must contact for quotes
Limited online case information or educational resources beyond general bankruptcy categories
No mention of alternative debt solutions (counseling, settlement, consolidation without bankruptcy) on their main messaging
Testimonials are self-selected; no independent third-party ratings or disciplinary history information provided
best_for: Individuals and business owners with complex multi-creditor debt situations exceeding tens of thousands of dollars
Cases previously rejected by other bankruptcy attorneys due to complexity or unusual circumstances
Middle Tennessee residents (Nashville, Cookeville, Upper Cumberland area) seeking in-person legal representation
diagnosis: Lefkovitz & Lefkovitz is best for individuals and business owners in Middle Tennessee facing complex, multi-creditor bankruptcy situations—especially cases involving multiple businesses, significant assets, or circumstances other attorneys have declined. The main caveat is that bankruptcy is a major financial decision with 7-10 year credit reporting consequences; potential clients should verify this firm explores all alternatives before filing and understand the long-term impact before proceeding.
services: Chapter 11 business bankruptcy filing and representation
In-depth legal research for difficult cases
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
